description_long: This is a tall, leafless deciduous tree with a straight, upright trunk and several thick, angular branches extending outward at irregular intervals. The bark appears rough and textured, with dark patches suggesting areas of damage or decay. The branches are mostly bare, with no visible foliage, giving the tree a stark, skeletal appearance. The trunk is relatively thick at the base and tapers as it rises, supporting the main branches that split off at various heights. The overall silhouette is asymmetrical, with some branches longer and more prominent than others. This type of tree is commonly found in urban parks, streets, or naturalized green spaces, especially in winter or after losing its leaves.
description: A tall, leafless tree with a rough, textured trunk and several thick, angular branches. The silhouette is stark and asymmetrical, with no visible foliage.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main trunk with three prominent branches extending left and right, and visible dark bark patches.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the angular branching structure and a large, dark patch on the lower right branch.
description_view_2: Rear view with the trunk and branches more aligned, showing the asymmetry and lack of foliage.
description_view_3: Oblique view emphasizing the tree's height and the irregular, jagged arrangement of branches.
